notes:
em = younger sibling (brother or sister). It can also mean a younger person in general, depending on who’s speaking.

As a noun, you often specify gender: em trai (younger brother), em gái (younger sister). For counting, use một người em (neutral) or đứa em (more casual, family tone).

It’s also a very common pronoun: a younger person calls themself em when talking to someone older, and the older person calls the younger one em. 

Strong memory link: pairs with anh / chị (older brother / older sister).

translation.en: I see you at the park.

exampleSentences: Anh luôn giúp em em làm bài tập về nhà mỗi tối.
Gia đình tôi có hai em em rất dễ thương và ngoan ngoãn.
Mỗi lần về quê, chị thường mang quà cho em em của mình.
exampleSentencesTranslated: He always helps his younger sibling do homework every night.
My family has two younger sibling who are very cute and well-behaved.
Every time she returns to her hometown, she often brings gifts for her younger sibling.

example.vi: Hai chị em em rất thân thiết với nhau.
example.en: My younger sisters are very close to each other.

definitions.vi: Từ dùng để chỉ những người em gái hoặc em trai, thường được lặp lại để nhấn mạnh số nhiều hoặc sự thân mật.
definitions.en: A term referring to younger siblings, often repeated for emphasis or affection.

translation.en: I like you to play with me.

notes_md:
**em** = younger sibling (brother or sister). It can also mean a younger person in general, depending on who’s speaking.

As a noun, you often specify gender: **em trai** (younger brother), **em gái** (younger sister). For counting, use **một người em** (neutral) or **đứa em** (more casual, family tone).

It’s also a very common pronoun: a younger person calls themself **em** when talking to someone older, and the older person calls the younger one **em**. 

Strong memory link: pairs with **anh / chị** (older brother / older sister).
